title: Character-Level Perturbations Disrupt LLM Watermarks
summary: A robustness study showing how small character-level perturbations can disrupt tokenization and bypass multiple LLM watermark detectors.
description: This project studies why existing LLM watermark evaluations can overestimate robustness. It focuses on character-level edits such as typos, deletion, and homoglyph substitutions, showing that tiny surface changes can cascade through tokenization and strongly reduce watermark detectability.
details: Defines realistic limited-detector threat models for watermark removal.
Analyzes how character edits disturb tokenization across representative watermarking schemes.
Introduces reference-detector-guided genetic search and compound character-level attacks.
Evaluates five watermarking schemes and two widely used LLM backbones.

title: OpenPlotAgent
summary: A human-in-the-loop AI plotting assistant for research figures, combining conversational chart generation, manual SVG editing, PDF export, and Git-tracked provenance.
description: OpenPlotAgent is an AI plotting workspace for researchers who want Overleaf-like iteration for figures. An agent explores data, writes plotting code, renders charts in a sandbox, and exposes every tool call. The user can then directly edit SVG elements, code, data, and properties before exporting publication-ready figures.
details: Spatial v2 interface with draggable chart, code, data, layers, property, and agent panels.
Conversational plotting plus manual visual takeover for colors, labels, legends, and axes.
Sandboxed Python execution with SVG/PDF export and journal-oriented figure defaults.
Git history and multi-level agent memory preserve provenance across plotting sessions.

title: PaperHarness
summary: A reproducibility harness that turns a paper and repository into an auditable reproduction kit: extracted facts, repo mapping, experiment plan, CLI, and report protocol.
description: PaperHarness compiles a paper and its code repository into an agent-operable reproduction kit. It maps paper claims and experiments to code entry points, records missing information, and generates a structured protocol that a coding agent can follow without repeatedly rediscovering the repository.
details: Generates `reprokit.yaml`, an agent skill, an experiment plan, and a report template.
Creates a `repro` CLI for inspection, validation, smoke tests, experiment runs, collection, and reporting.
Surfaces missing datasets, checkpoints, commands, mappings, and review-required uncertainties.
Gives Codex-style agents a stable operating surface for paper reproduction attempts.

title: Stealing Watermarks of Large Language Models via Mixed Integer Programming
summary: Code release for stealing LLM watermark green lists with mixed integer programming, plus frequency baselines and watermark-removal evaluation scripts.
description: This repository contains the artifact for the ACSAC 2024 work on stealing LLM watermarks. It collects watermarked text, recovers hidden green lists with mixed integer programming, compares against frequency-based baselines, and evaluates watermark removal using the stolen information.
details: Supports unigram and multi-key watermark stealing experiments.
Core MIP implementation lives in `greenlist_inversion_plus.py`, with frequency baselines in `greenlist_inversion_sta.py`.
Includes scripts for collecting watermarked text, exporting ground-truth green lists, and printing stealing results.
Includes watermark removal evaluation through `wm_wiper.py` and related test scripts.
